member.office_address: 2004 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-4307

form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Please fill out the below form to request that Congresswoman Fletcher submit a specific fiscal year 2027 (FY27) appropriations request. If you would like her to submit an original request, which is a request with no current lead sponsor, please contact Brendon Maloney our Legislative Director at Brendon.Maloney@mail.house.gov. Please note that filling out this form does not guarantee the Congresswoman will sign on to the appropriations request submitted. 

The House Appropriations Committee has released guidance for the FY27 appropriations cycle. Please see the following link for the FY27 : https://appropriations.house.gov/fy27-member-requests/fy27-guidance-overview . Please review this guidance before submitting an application. 

The tentative deadline for submissions is 5:00 PM CST Friday, March 6, 2026. 

If you have any further questions, please call Congresswoman Fletcher's office at (202) 225-2571.

sections.description: Information about the organization submitting the request and the general nature of the proposal.

fields.label: Appropriations Subcommittee
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs
Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the jurisdiction of the CJS subcommittee as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Is this a Programmatic Request or Report Language Request?
fields.rationale: The reference context defines this as a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Which office is leading the accompanying letter?
fields.help_text: Identify the Congressional office leading the request letter.
fields.draft_value: Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: The reference values identify these members as the leads for the house version of the accompanying letter.

fields.label: Please list the staff contact and email for that office:
fields.help_text: Staff information for the lead Congressional office.
fields.rationale: Staff-specific contact information is not provided in the context and requires submitter-specific knowledge.

sections.description: Specifics regarding requests for funding levels within agency programs.

fields.label: For Programmatic Requests- Agency/Office/Program Title
fields.rationale: This identifies the specific agency and account as requested in the instructions.

fields.label: Description of Programmatic Request
fields.draft_value:
* Requesting $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to sustain U.S. leadership in space exploration and scientific discovery.
* Supports essential missions across Earth Science, Planetary Science, and Astrophysics based on nonpartisan, peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities.
* Protects the domestic aerospace workforce and innovation pipeline from the disruptions caused by prior year funding uncertainty.
* Ensures the U.S. sets the norms for 21st-century exploration as international competition in space science rapidly intensifies.
fields.rationale: Directly utilizes the $9 billion ask amount and core advocacy talking points regarding leadership, workforce stability, and international competition.

fields.label: Program Funding Level
options: Support the President's FY 2025 Budget Request
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding amount ($9 billion) which is best captured by this selection.

fields.label: If you selected "Support total funding of $______ amount, what is the amount you are requesting?
fields.help_text: Enter the specific dollar amount requested for FY27.
fields.rationale: The specific funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate is $9 billion.

fields.label: Does this programmatic request have a direct connection or impact in TX-07?
fields.rationale: NASA Science has invested nearly $5 million in TX-7 over recent fiscal years, primarily through grants to William Marsh Rice University for research in planetary habitability and aurora physics.

fields.label: Describe TX-07 connection or impact:
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science obligated $4,932,838 to TX-07 institutions between FY2023 and FY2025, supporting high-tech research and STEM jobs in Houston.
* William Marsh Rice University leads world-class research with over $14.7M in recent grants, including a $6.6M project investigating planetary habitability.
* Local technology small businesses, including Infratec Infrared LLC and PC Vision Inc, provide specialized capabilities to the NASA mission pipeline.
* These investments fuel the local innovation economy and inspire the next generation of aerospace professionals in the Houston region.
fields.rationale: Cites specific local spending data for TX-07, including the $4.9M total obligations, Rice University's significant grant role, and local contract recipients.

sections.description: Specifics regarding requests for report or bill language.

fields.label: For Language Requests- Agency/Office/Program Title
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request, not a language request.

fields.label: Exact Language Request
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request, not a language request.

fields.label: Explanation for Language Request
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this is a programmatic request and that no report or bill language is being requested.

fields.label: Does this language request have a direct connection or impact in TX-07?
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request, not a language request.

fields.label: Description of TX-07 connection or impact
fields.rationale: This field is associated with the language request section, which does not apply to this programmatic funding request.

top_grants.description: Guiding question: how to acquire  sustain  and nurture life-essential chemical ingredients on the surface of rocky planets? Building a habitable environment on inner solar system rocky surfaces requires the life-essential chemical ingredients (c  N  o  h  S  and p) to journey through a series of astronomical  planetary  geological  and geochemical processes during planet formation and early planetary evolution. However  in most studies only one or few of these steps are investigated in isolation as conditions of habitability and the consequences of multiple pathways that may be feasible are not rigorously explored. Similarly  most studies tracking the impact of prebiotic processes considered the origin of water alone  and comprehensive studies on a combination of life-essential elements are lacking. In order to evaluate the pathways that may lead to early habitable conditions in multiple planetary surfaces but sustenance only in a subset  an interdisciplinary approach to study the early cycles of all key ingredients are necessary. This can 8 nai node proposal aims to constrain the pathways that lead to the acquisition  sustenance  and nurturing of the life-essential chemical elements at the surface and near-surface environments of rocky  differentiated planetary bodies leading to habitability and potentially driving the formation of prebiotically-relevant organic molecule precursors within the first billion years of a planet S evolution. Our team members bring extensive expertise in astrophysics  geology  geochemistry  geophysics  fluid and atmospheric dynamics  climate science  and organic chemistry; an overarching goal is to trace the life-essential elements from protoplanetary disks to early prebiotic molecules on the surfaces of rocky planets  with a particular emphasis on the feedbacks among interior  surface  and atmospheric processes. We will use observations from our own solar system in terms of comparative planetology but will also construct possible pathways that may lead to thermo-chemical habitability  which can be utilized for exploration of other solar systems in search for life. The proposed research complements the activities of the can 7 teams because none of the current teams are investigating the cycles of the key ingredients during differentiation and early tectono-magmatic and geochemical evolution of rocky planets. Our proposal will bring in the unique interdisciplinary perspective of cycles of key ingredients of life through deep time from geologic  planetary  geophysical  and geochemical perspectives. Given the interdependent nature of the proposed activities among seemingly disparate fields of research  the scope of this proposal can only be supported through an  institute without walls . Relevance to the goals of the astrobiology program: the proposed investigation is relevant for several of the goals of the astrobiology program as outlined in the 2015 strategy including the central themes of  constructing habitable worlds  and  identifying  exploring  and characterizing environments for habitability and biosignatures . Our proposal will directly address questions such as  how does the story of Earth  in particular it S past inform us about how the climates  atmospheric compositions  interiors  and biospheres of planets can co-evolve?  Because our team will study the interactions of interior  atmosphere  and climate in the first billion years of a rocky planets evolution  keeping in mind the history of the Earth as an analog. Our proposal will also directly address questions such as what are the fundamental ingredients and processes that define a habitable environment?  Because we will track the fate of the life-essential elements through the fundamental processes of planet formation and early geologic and biogeochemical evolution with the goal of outlining possible paths that are or are not favorable in finally harboring the cohnsp species at the planetary surfaces.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Investigating cycles of life's essential elements for planetary habitability.

top_grants.description: Our objective is better understanding of the plasma physics processes responsible for auroral phenomena  and construction of a comprehensive physics-based numerical coupled model of aurora that includes ionospheric  thermosphere.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Researching plasma physics to model the Earth's aurora.

top_grants.description: Modeling hard x-ray bursts and giant flares from magnetars
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Simulating giant flares and hard x-ray bursts from magnetars.

top_grants.description: Evaluating geochronologic complexity and impactor diversity of highland impactites
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Determining the history and sources of ancient planetary impact events.

top_grants.description: The proposed work seeks to aid in the characterization of known exoplanets and will contribute to the detection  verification  and characterization of new exoplanets.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Detecting, verifying, and characterizing distant exoplanets.

top_grants.description: The roses funding opportunity goals are: execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress. Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond. Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Funding balanced science supporting Moon, Mars exploration, and planetary defense.

top_grants.description: 24-pimove24-0024 constraining the origin and timescale of lunar mg-suite magmatism
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Determining the timing and source of ancient lunar volcanic activity.
